# Support Tool Comparison Matrix (May 2026)

## Feature matrix

| Feature | Plain | Pylon | Help Scout | Front | Intercom |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Best fit** | B2B SaaS, dev-first | B2B Slack-heavy | SMB email-first | Multi-channel SMB | B2C/growth-stage |
| **Pricing model** | Seat-based | Seat-based (opaque) | Contact-based | Seat-based | Seat + outcome |
| **Starting price** | $50/agent/mo | ~$75/agent/mo (est.) | $50/mo (1,000 contacts) | $19/agent/mo | $74/agent/mo |
| **Email inbox** | Yes (primary) | Secondary | Yes (primary) | Yes (primary) | Yes |
| **Slack Connect inbox** | Yes (best-in-class) | Yes (primary) | No | No | No |
| **In-app chat widget** | Yes (via embed) | No | Yes | Yes | Yes (Messenger) |
| **AI deflection** | No (beta AI assist) | Pylon AI (copilot) | AI Assist (copilot) | Front AI (copilot) | Fin 2.0 (autonomous) |
| **Linear integration** | Native (best) | Native (basic) | Via Zapier | Via Zapier | Via Zapier |
| **Slack alert on breach** | Yes | Yes | No (Zapier needed) | Yes | Yes |
| **CSAT collection** | No (integration) | No (integration) | Yes (Pro plan) | Yes (built-in) | Yes (built-in) |
| **API quality** | Excellent (GraphQL) | Good (REST) | Good (REST) | Good (REST) | Good (REST) |
| **Knowledge base** | Yes (add-on) | No | Yes (included) | Yes (included) | Yes (Help Center) |
| **Multi-language** | Partial | No | Yes | Yes | Yes (45 languages) |

## Decision tree

```
Start: What is your primary support channel?
├── Email → Go to Q2
├── Slack Connect (enterprise customers) → Consider Plain or Pylon
│   └── Need strong Linear integration? → Plain (native) > Pylon (basic)
└── In-app chat → Go to Q3

Q2: What is your B2B/B2C posture?
├── B2B SaaS (< 5K customers) → Plain or Help Scout
│   └── Need AI deflection? → Add Intercom (or migrate to Intercom)
└── B2C / consumer → Intercom or Freshdesk+Ari

Q3: What is your conversation volume?
├── < 500/month → Help Scout or Plain (Starter)
├── 500-5K/month → Plain (Pro) or Intercom (Starter)
└── > 5K/month → Intercom or Front (enterprise)

Q4: Is AI deflection a priority?
├── Yes, autonomous (high volume, good KB) → Intercom + Fin 2.0
├── Yes, copilot only → Front AI or Help Scout AI Assist
└── No → Plain or Pylon
```

## Key takeaways

- Plain is the default recommendation for developer-first B2B SaaS with < 5K conversations/month and a need for Slack Connect or Linear integration.
- Intercom is the only tool with autonomous AI deflection (Fin 2.0); recommended when volume > 2K/month AND knowledge base > 50 articles.
- Help Scout's 2025 contact-based pricing pivot makes it less competitive for growing teams; evaluate total contact database size before recommending.
- Front is the strongest for multi-channel teams that need sophisticated SLA reporting and CSAT collection.
- Pylon is optimal for the specific use case of managing many Slack Connect channels as the primary support surface.
